diagrams.title: SCAN Disk Scheduling Algorithm
diagrams.description: Shows the disk head moving like an elevator from inner track to outer track and back, servicing all requests in path
elements: Disk tracks numbered from inner (0) to outer (N)
Request queue at various track positions
Head movement path showing forward and reverse directions
diagrams.purpose: Understand how SCAN algorithm services requests in both directions, providing more uniform response time than SSTF

diagrams.title: Indexed File Allocation
diagrams.description: Shows file data blocks connected through an index block that contains pointers to all data blocks
elements: File directory entry pointing to index block
diagrams.purpose: Understand how indexed allocation enables direct random access to any file block without traversing the entire file

diagrams.title: Bit Vector Free Space Management
diagrams.description: Shows bitmap representation of disk blocks with 0 for free and 1 for allocated blocks
elements: Bit vector array with corresponding bits
Example showing allocation of contiguous blocks
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how bit vector tracks free space efficiently, allowing quick calculation of available blocks

diagrams.title: RAID Levels Comparison
diagrams.description: Diagram comparing RAID 0 (striping), RAID 1 (mirroring), and RAID 5 (parity) data distribution
diagrams.purpose: Understand different RAID configurations and their trade-offs between performance, storage efficiency, and fault tolerance
